tags.notes: Part of Kisite-Mpunguti Marine Park with 250+ fish species and 40+ coral varieties. Coordinates from Wasini House Reef on Zentacle (nearest mapped site in Mpunguti reserve).
Protected marine park with 16 dive sites, coral gardens with 150+ coral species, 250+ fish species. Coordinates from MPAtlas park center.
Known for whale sharks (Nov-Feb), turtle communities, vibrant coral gardens. Coordinates from Kongo Reef on Zentacle (central Diani reef area).
76m cargo ship scuttled 2002, sits upright on sand, penetration lines installed, moray eels and barracudas. Wikipedia coordinates: 4°01'04"S 39°46'28"E, confirmed by Zentacle.
Mangrove-lined tidal creek with strong currents, large barracuda and trevally schools, drift diving recommended. Coordinates from cruising guide (3°55'S, 39°45'E).
Reef wall with drop-offs, crevices with octopuses and lionfish, accessible from shore at shallow depths. Coordinates from Coral Trait Database.

Dramatic underwater caves and tunnels with two-current convergence point, dolphins and reef sharks, experienced divers only. Coordinates based on Shimoni harbor position (dive site accessed from Shimoni).
Part of Kisite-Mpunguti reef system, coral reef with depth 12-18m typical range. Coordinates from Kisite Island center (reef surrounds island).
Four mangrove-covered islets near Kisite-Mpunguti, 360+ fish species and 45+ coral types in region. Coordinates from geographic databases.

Partly underwater caves with giant groupers (up to 363kg), suitable for all experience levels, white sand surroundings. Coordinates estimated from Watamu Marine Park boundary (dive site is within/near the park, accessed by boat from Watamu).
